i've finally gotten over my aesthetic aversion to polynomials and it makes galois theory so much easier

it helped to figure out that irreducible polynomials never have roots in common, so irr polys kinda form a mirror of the algebraic closure of the underlying field

text:
there's a few other things that add way more structure than I realized, which makes polynomials feel less like this giant amorphous unstructured soup

this isomorphism between the polynomial ring quotient a min polynomial and the simple extension of the base field by a root

raw.full_text:
there's a few other things that add way more structure than I realized, which makes polynomials feel less like this giant amorphous unstructured soup

text: irreducible polynomials with degree higher than 1 indicates roots outside the field! If your roots where in the field, you'd be able to factor them out with a degree 1 term! And then you wouldn't be irreducible!

raw.full_text: irreducible polynomials with degree higher than 1 indicates roots outside the field! If your roots where in the field, you'd be able to factor them out with a degree 1 term! And then you wouldn't be irreducible!

text: a polynomial that's irreducible over a field will also either be degree 1 or None of its roots are in the field, corollary of previous statement

text: like, voltage as "potential of E field", but thats actually diff from what's happening in a battery, and when you have an inductor the e fields created that move charges are non-conservative unlike with static charges, and so some of the concepts overloaded into V don't apply/

raw.full_text: like, voltage as "potential of E field", but thats actually diff from what's happening in a battery, and when you have an inductor the e fields created that move charges are non-conservative unlike with static charges, and so some of the concepts overloaded into V don't apply/

text: x-ray tubes seem pretty simple, you have a filament circuit which heats up the material you're emitting electrons from (cathode), and the tube circuit which creates a voltage across between the cathode and receiving node (anode), and you smash electrons into the anode til x-rays https://t.co/181XFMp19b

raw.full_text: x-ray tubes seem pretty simple, you have a filament circuit which heats up the material you're emitting electrons from (cathode), and the tube circuit which creates a voltage across between the cathode and receiving node (anode), and you smash electrons into the anode til x-rays https://t.co/181XFMp19b

text: if you have a higher voltage across the tube, that means bigger e-field which means electrons reach reach higher velocity when they collide, so the photons you create will be higher energy/freq. if you drove an x-ray tube at too low a voltage you'd get ultraviolent EM, not x-ray

microwave oven transformers (MOTs), neon sign transformers (NSTs), and flybacks are the three kinds of transformers I'm acquainted with

flybacks are interesting because they refer to both a different construction, and also a different way of powering them https://t.co/JAAF6RQ9hl

text:
so normally you run a transformer with AC, and the sinusoidal voltage makes sure there's always changing current and so the induction magic is always powering the other side

But with a flyback transformer you just send DC pulses to run it https://t.co/rlcRIy5cTj

raw.full_text:
so normally you run a transformer with AC, and the sinusoidal voltage makes sure there's always changing current and so the induction magic is always powering the other side

But with a flyback transformer you just send DC pulses to run it https://t.co/rlcRIy5cTj

text:
pulse DC on, changing field, current induced in secondary in one direction, DC off, field collapses, current induced in other direction

so you do pulsing DC and get imperfectly sinusoidal AC out, though often I think people add a diode to rectify the output back to DC

so you do pulsing DC and get imperfectly sinusoidal AC out, though often I think people add a diode to rectify the output back to DC

text:
im loosely calling whatever the logic of one layer feeding into the next the "programming language" for that layer. Like how I might use system verilog to describe a hardware system then assembly for its machine code and then higher level languages in that

and at any level, if the dynamics/logic of that level aren't clear to you, you just see structure at a higher level without a sense of how the previous level created it. And I'm guessing it's only in those sitch where people use the term emergence, and it's less used when they grasp the connection between the layers

text: cutting the chase a bit: i think the biggest thing that people get wrong about the way self-deception works, is people assert "if one is self-deceiving, there just isn't any incriminating/contradictory evidence in their mind/experience, it's necessarily walled away and not accessible, so there's never any point in probing yourself or others about this"

raw.full_text: cutting the chase a bit: i think the biggest thing that people get wrong about the way self-deception works, is people assert "if one is self-deceiving, there just isn't any incriminating/contradictory evidence in their mind/experience, it's necessarily walled away and not accessible, so there's never any point in probing yourself or others about this"

text: this is tapping into some mix of a spatial model (real thoughts/feelings/motives are sectioned off in a certain area, unconscious is guarding it not letting them out) and a "forgery" model (the only things "you" experience are forgeries/confabulations your unconscious gives u)

raw.full_text: this is tapping into some mix of a spatial model (real thoughts/feelings/motives are sectioned off in a certain area, unconscious is guarding it not letting them out) and a "forgery" model (the only things "you" experience are forgeries/confabulations your unconscious gives u)

text:
self-deception works by not indexing the incriminating experiences into your broader self structure. in a "parts" model of the mind, consciousness is communication between parts and the symbolic self is the accumulated paper trail, and self-deception is fucking with the paper trail trying to remove incriminting evidence from it, but except in super extreme cases it doesn't seem to actually get wiped from your experience

https://t.co/ByUbJDlwOj

your SNS is typically v active when you're in a threat/danger mode, but it's also v active when you're having a blast playing sports

SNS activation basically comes down to making the body Ready To Rumble for whatever

raw.full_text:
your SNS is typically v active when you're in a threat/danger mode, but it's also v active when you're having a blast playing sports

SNS activation basically comes down to making the body Ready To Rumble for whatever

text:
SNS doesn't even control cortisol, that's a completely different channel

a lot of canonical threat response stuff is controlled by the periaqueductal gray (PAG), things like yelling/howling, instinctive defensive postures, pain receptors dimming

raw.full_text:
SNS doesn't even control cortisol, that's a completely different channel

a lot of canonical threat response stuff is controlled by the periaqueductal gray (PAG), things like yelling/howling, instinctive defensive postures, pain receptors dimming

text: also, the parasympathetic nervous system (PNS) which the dorsal ventral system is supposed to be a subpart of, doesn't control a lot of the classic Freeze behavior. like the kind of "tonic immobility" that's common, that's PAG not PNS

text:
the good faith game: 3 people, spotter, jester, judge, looking at a city skyline

jester flips coin secretly to decide good or bad faith. spotter picks building and tries to point it out with language with only 30 sec. jester is only trying to get it if coin says good faith, otherwise he's trying to stall. if good faith and gets it, spotter and jester get a point. if 30 sec with no correct guess, spotter decides if he's going to charge with bad faith or not. judge decides guilty or not based on honest opinion. spotter gets points for successful conviction of bad faith, and loses points for failed conviction

might need some workshopping but feels like a concept!

text:
lit!

on the illegibility point, i think all the legit points that underly the impulse to resisting legibility are in accord with the CA

i think if being on twitter at all is within one's risk profile for dark forest violence and harrassment, the CA doesn't make it worse

also while attempting to self-describe and understand one's self, you can always revise your descriptions as needed, and add more details. writing blog post length expositions of the discourse across hundreds/thousands of tweets is a compression, but not the destructive kind of "make 5 words".

i think it only time it kinda sort makes sense to categorically avoid self-description is when you're stomach deep in egregore possession and your mind feels like an active warzone, and if you can't stablize that to a point where self-description is safe on the time scale of the existence of this group, you've got bigger more fundamental problems

human adversarial examples seem to point to the fact that people also get confused when you go far enough off distribution, and sometimes it's "easy" to go off dist, like with writing numbers, but hard in others, with the animals. or hard in that, easy to arrange the pixels, but you aren't going to find that thing out in the world.

text:
ben clearly isn't arguing that it secretly makes sense to be bad with money and food, and that you too will eventually realize why it's secretly rational to do so

i think both you and ben notice that the food problem is v solvable unless you have something really weird going on with you. i think you notice empirically that there are people who do seem to have things wrong with them and are in fact failing to feed their families

from how you described your intent, i think you don't think that you can rationally inform these people, but you do notice that sometimes when people say "guys, don't be retarded, solve your problem by doing X" the people with the weird problem can be shamed into solving that specific problem for some amount of time. i think that does happen, and you are right that you will change some people's food strategies some amount. but i think

ben is, among other things, pointing out that it's worth taking some time to ponder why it is that lots of people seem like they can only be shamed into staying alive and can't themselves be motivated to stay alive. not because that's relevant to how it makes sense for you to feed your family, but it seems broadly relevant for figuring out how to move thru the world

im now calling the main way I think this persistence works "phantom proxy possession" 

there's an asymmetry between badness needed to make a wound vs the badness needed to keep a wound open. But our social env are still fucked up, but just less intensely, only enough to press our buttons in the same patterns to keep our wounds alive

text:
i think some cases of this are to avoid thinking about the social conflicts actually embeded in their envs

like, kanta axe murderer is obvs an okay time to lie, and it's also important that people who you want to have a truth telling reputation with understand the sorts of situations you will and won't lie in, but to create any more shared clarity around that then "of course I'd lie to the ace murderer" need to actually have open conversations about the sorts of adversaries and conflicts you in fact encounter and are likely to encounter in day-to-day life 

and if you're blocked from having those conversations and also very concerned with a reputation for being truthful, I can see how you might insist "I can only be trusted if I ALWAYS tell the truth!" but you'll still be in hostile situations were there good reasons not to and feel that pressure and probs lie to yourself about when you are and aren't truthful

mb I just didn't get your original claim. Here's what I thought your graphic was saying:
 groups/egregores name things and make language. In this model, having language for something is a pre-requisite for being able to think about something. If you join the rats and get the termed "trapped priors" you can  a) talk about that terrain with people and b) think about it yourself.

The whole part of "if you get kicked out of the egregores, you either need to drop to a lower level of awareness or make your own language" seems like it straight forwardly means if the new rat gets kicked out of the rats, he both has no people to talk to about the terrain ("trapped priors? Jesse what the hell are you talking about?") and ALSO he can no longer think about the terrain and can't even use "trapped priors" in his own thinking to talk about it. And so unless he comes up with a new term for that terrain he's shit out of luck

Is that the basic model?
